name: Mars
cat: Food & Beverage / Confectionery, Pet Care, Snacking

political.s: Mars leans right politically with $683K raised and 100% directed to Republicans according to FEC data. The company spent $2M lobbying in 2024 and $1M in 2025 (year-to-date), primarily on food/agriculture issues and regulatory matters.

charity.s: Mars commits substantial resources to charitable giving, including $1 billion for sustainability through its Sustainable in a Generation initiative and $1 billion for smallholder farmers. The company donated 33 million meals since 2021 and provided $42+ million in COVID-19 relief.
charity.focus: Sustainable agriculture, poverty alleviation, animal welfare, disaster relief, community development

environment.s: Mars achieved a 1.9% reduction in GHG emissions in 2024 against a 2015 baseline while growing business 69%. The company faces criticism for cocoa sourcing in protected forests and palm oil supply chain issues, though it has pledged net zero by 2050 and pioneered climate-smart agriculture practices.

labor.s: Mars reported $27.4 million in environmental/labor penalties since 2000. The company faced OSHA citations for a 2022 incident where workers fell into a molten chocolate tank due to inadequate safety training. Mars emphasizes competitive wages—higher than industry peers—and operates with transparent organizational structures.

dei.s: Mars achieves a 4.3/5 DEI rating on Glassdoor (27.6% above manufacturing sector average) and reports 46% gender balance in leadership. The company operates five global Associate Resource Groups and launched the #HereToBeHeard campaign engaging 10,000 women across 88 countries.

animals.s: Mars maintains an animal research policy prohibiting harm in day-to-day product development but permits limited testing on rats, mice, and fish for new ingredients and regulatory compliance. The company established the Mars Research Review Board in 2007 to oversee ethical standards, though PETA lists Mars on its animal testing list.

guns.s: Mars does not manufacture, sell, or distribute firearms or firearms-related products as part of its business operations.

privacy.s: No public privacy policy or data privacy practices found in search results. As a private company in food/pet care, privacy considerations are not a primary business concern.

execPay.s: Mars executive compensation averages $232,919 annually. CEO compensation estimated at $325,000+ based on available data. Limited public disclosure available for executive pay ratios given private company status; compensation data suggests alignment with industry standards.

fec.committeeName: MAKING A RESPONSIBLE STAND FOR HOUSEHOLDS IN AMERICA PAC

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
